notes: User cancels a long in-flight aider turn with the interrupt key, exercising the user-ESC-interrupt primitive specifically. aider's cancel key is Ctrl-C, not ESC (aider has no ESC-to-interrupt binding; scenario-meanings names Ctrl-C for aider). The user-observable signal is ready→working→ready with state NOT sticking in working post-interrupt. SCOPE / observes:partial — the ready→working→ready arc IS observed, but only via the GENERIC turn-end path: a Ctrl-C-cancelled aider turn writes NO `> Tokens:` line (the turn aborted before any model call reported usage) and no error blockquote, so the parser's two explicit turn-closers never fire; the turn is closed by IdleFlush, which synthesizes a bare turn_done once the markdown transcript has been quiet for aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms), and the classifier settles working→ready via its generic 'agent finished turn → ready' rule — the SAME path every clean aider turn takes. irrlicht therefore observes the interrupt only as a generic turn-end: there is NO interrupt-IDENTITY signal. aider's markdown carries no `[Request interrupted by user]` marker (that is claudecode-specific), so the interrupt-aware machinery (IsUserInterrupt / sticky LastWasUserInterrupt / classifier rule 3 with reason 'user ESC interrupt') is never engaged; the interrupt is invisible AS an interrupt. AIDER STRUCTURE: aider is the lone FilesUnderCWD adapter — .aider.chat.history.md is markdown parsed by a RawParser. RECIPE CONSTRAINT: the interrupt step sends tmux Ctrl-C and is NOT followed by wait_turn (the cancelled turn yields no `> Tokens:` line and would hang wait_turn); the recipe sleeps after the interrupt so IdleFlush + the daemon's 5s staleWorking refresh tick land the settle before teardown. Effective post-interrupt settle latency is ~max(1500ms IdleFlush floor, 5s SessionDetector staleWorking refresh tick) — interrupt_settle tolerates that window rather than demanding an immediate flip. SESSION-IDENTITY (same workaround as aider/multi-turn-conversation): aider's transcript is lazy, so at launch the process scanner emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready — session_birth only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' and legitimately matches that pre-session. The turn arc must land on the per-cwd rollout session: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); the pid_bind, interrupt_settle, and teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording (the cancelled essay text), per-turn token counts, how much of the essay streamed before Ctrl-C landed, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance); the cancelled turn contributes zero tokens/cost (no `> Tokens:` line). Stable structurally: a ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id that does NOT stick in working post-interrupt and never enters waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (aider's transcript is lazy, so this is the launch-time proc-<PID> pre-session)
The long-essay prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)

aider binds the PID at session birth (~ms after launch, BEFORE the first prompt), so anchor to recording start and do not pin same_session_as — same shape as aider/basic-turn and aider/interrupted-turn

After the Ctrl-C interrupt the cancelled turn writes no turn-done marker; IdleFlush settles the rollout session working→ready from transcript-quiet alone (the GENERIC turn-end path, NOT an interrupt-identity signal — aider's markdown has no interrupt marker) — state does not stick in working, and never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the interrupt settles → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
